Angular 22 ships Angular Aria as a stable API the same week the European Accessibility Act becomes enforceable; here is what you actually need to wire up.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

The European Accessibility Act enforcement date is June 28, 2026. That is today. And Angular 22, released June 3, happens to be the first version that ships Angular Aria at stable status, graduating it out of developer preview. The timing is coincidental, but the pairing is useful. If you are running v22 and you still have role and live-region state wired through raw setAttribute calls or hand-rolled services, you are about to see why that approach creates more surface area than it solves.
The Pattern That Shows Up in Every Enterprise Codebase
The common shortcut is a shared AriaService that wraps document.getElementById and toggles attributes imperatively. It works for one component. It breaks at scale because nothing in the component tree knows about that service's side effects, the call order is timing-dependent, and tests have to stub the DOM directly.
Here is the weaker pattern:
// aria.service.ts — the kind that ends up in every shared/services folder
@Injectable({ providedIn: 'root' })
export class AriaService {
announce(message: string): void {…
